required_measurements: unpowered resistance between power and ground
continuity from connector ground to exposed ground
driver output voltage with dummy load

adapter_circuits.name: fused low-side MOSFET switch
adapter_circuits.use_when: controlling a DC motor, fan, pump, relay, or LED strip from a switch or controller
must_include: gate/base resistor if driven by controller

adapter_circuits.name: buck/boost regulator or protected power breakout
adapter_circuits.use_when: source voltage does not exactly match the salvaged load/module rating

wiring_steps: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos
power the reused block from a current-limited supply at the lowest plausible rated voltage
verify the block output function before connecting it to any other module
wire the motor/load through a fuse or current limit first
add a MOSFET/transistor driver if a controller or switch cannot handle load current
add flyback protection for inductive motors, pumps, relays, or coils
verify switch/button continuity and debounce behavior before using it as an input panel
mount the new build so wires have strain relief and cannot short
record a first-power test, thermal check, and final output function
mechanical_steps: keep strain relief on harvested wires and connectors
mount moving parts so wires cannot touch blades, gears, belts, rails, or hot surfaces
label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification
do_not_connect_until: rail voltage is measured under current limit
battery or mains hazards are cleared

integration_contract.target_build: USB fume extractor or bench cooling fan
integration_contract.output_function: moves air for cooling, drying, or solder-fume capture
unknowns_to_close: unpowered resistance between power and ground
continuity from connector ground to exposed ground
driver output voltage with dummy load
integration_contract.first_demo: show airflow from a current-limited supply and record current draw after one minute

capture_prompts: part markings on drivers, regulators, controllers, and sensors
new build wiring before first power-up
motor/load label, wire colors, and shaft/mechanical mount
measurement_prompts: unpowered resistance between power and ground
continuity from connector ground to exposed ground
driver output voltage with dummy load
review_prompts: operator confirms reused block function
operator confirms voltage/current rating before power
operator records final build outcome and value

stop_conditions: input voltage or polarity cannot be identified
power-to-ground resistance suggests a short
load current exceeds the wire, connector, switch, driver, or supply rating
module overheats, smells, smokes, or pulls unexpected current
battery chemistry/protection is unknown or the pack is damaged

proof_fields: value recovered or replacement cost avoided

honesty: image or text can propose reuse, but power and pin certainty comes from measurements
prefer low-voltage modules, connectors, motors, sensors, UI parts, and enclosures
do not reuse unknown battery packs, mains sections, or high-voltage modules without a separate safety procedure

adapter_circuits.name: fused low-side MOSFET switch
adapter_circuits.use_when: controlling a DC motor, fan, pump, relay, or LED strip from a switch or controller
must_include: gate/base resistor if driven by controller

adapter_circuits.name: sensor breakout harness

wiring_steps: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos
power the reused block from a current-limited supply at the lowest plausible rated voltage
verify the block output function before connecting it to any other module
wire the motor/load through a fuse or current limit first
add a MOSFET/transistor driver if a controller or switch cannot handle load current
add flyback protection for inductive motors, pumps, relays, or coils
secure belts, rails, gears, and limit switches before powered motion tests
verify switch/button continuity and debounce behavior before using it as an input panel
mount the new build so wires have strain relief and cannot short
record a first-power test, thermal check, and final output function
mechanical_steps: keep strain relief on harvested wires and connectors
mount moving parts so wires cannot touch blades, gears, belts, rails, or hot surfaces
label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification
mark original gear, belt, and limit-switch positions before removing the mechanism
do_not_connect_until: rail voltage is measured under current limit
battery or mains hazards are cleared

adapter_circuits.name: fused low-side MOSFET switch
adapter_circuits.use_when: controlling a DC motor, fan, pump, relay, or LED strip from a switch or controller
must_include: gate/base resistor if driven by controller

adapter_circuits.name: buck/boost regulator or protected power breakout
adapter_circuits.use_when: source voltage does not exactly match the salvaged load/module rating

adapter_circuits.name: motion-and-light fixture harness
adapter_circuits.use_when: a scanner, printer, or camera assembly provides a rail, light bar, limit switch, or optical sensor

wiring_steps: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos
power the reused block from a current-limited supply at the lowest plausible rated voltage
verify the block output function before connecting it to any other module
wire the motor/load through a fuse or current limit first
add a MOSFET/transistor driver if a controller or switch cannot handle load current
add flyback protection for inductive motors, pumps, relays, or coils
secure belts, rails, gears, and limit switches before powered motion tests
test the light bar separately with current limiting before coupling it to motion
home the rail at low speed against a verified limit switch before full-travel tests
mount the camera, sensor, or item holder so travel cannot pull on the harness
mechanical_steps: keep strain relief on harvested wires and connectors
mount moving parts so wires cannot touch blades, gears, belts, rails, or hot surfaces
label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification
keep the scanner/printer rail straight and preserve the original belt, pulley, or carriage alignment
add a physical end stop and mark the safe travel distance before powered motion
mount light/camera/sensor modules so focus distance and illumination angle are repeatable
do_not_connect_until: rail voltage is measured under current limit
battery or mains hazards are cleared

required_measurements: unpowered resistance between power and ground
continuity from connector ground to exposed ground
driver output voltage with dummy load

adapter_circuits.name: fused low-side MOSFET switch
adapter_circuits.use_when: controlling a DC motor, fan, pump, relay, or LED strip from a switch or controller
must_include: gate/base resistor if driven by controller

adapter_circuits.name: buck/boost regulator or protected power breakout
adapter_circuits.use_when: source voltage does not exactly match the salvaged load/module rating

wiring_steps: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos
power the reused block from a current-limited supply at the lowest plausible rated voltage
verify the block output function before connecting it to any other module
wire the motor/load through a fuse or current limit first
add a MOSFET/transistor driver if a controller or switch cannot handle load current
add flyback protection for inductive motors, pumps, relays, or coils
secure belts, rails, gears, and limit switches before powered motion tests
verify switch/button continuity and debounce behavior before using it as an input panel
mount the new build so wires have strain relief and cannot short
record a first-power test, thermal check, and final output function
mechanical_steps: keep strain relief on harvested wires and connectors
mount moving parts so wires cannot touch blades, gears, belts, rails, or hot surfaces
label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification
mark original gear, belt, and limit-switch positions before removing the mechanism
do_not_connect_until: rail voltage is measured under current limit
battery or mains hazards are cleared

label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification

battery or mains hazards are cleared

required_measurements: unpowered resistance between power and ground
continuity from connector ground to exposed ground
driver output voltage with dummy load

adapter_circuits.name: fused low-side MOSFET switch
adapter_circuits.use_when: controlling a DC motor, fan, pump, relay, or LED strip from a switch or controller
must_include: gate/base resistor if driven by controller

adapter_circuits.name: buck/boost regulator or protected power breakout
adapter_circuits.use_when: source voltage does not exactly match the salvaged load/module rating

wiring_steps: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos
power the reused block from a current-limited supply at the lowest plausible rated voltage
verify the block output function before connecting it to any other module
wire the motor/load through a fuse or current limit first
add a MOSFET/transistor driver if a controller or switch cannot handle load current
add flyback protection for inductive motors, pumps, relays, or coils
verify switch/button continuity and debounce behavior before using it as an input panel
mount the new build so wires have strain relief and cannot short
record a first-power test, thermal check, and final output function
mechanical_steps: keep strain relief on harvested wires and connectors
mount moving parts so wires cannot touch blades, gears, belts, rails, or hot surfaces
label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification
do_not_connect_until: rail voltage is measured under current limit
battery or mains hazards are cleared

label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification

battery or mains hazards are cleared

wiring_steps: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos
power the reused block from a current-limited supply at the lowest plausible rated voltage
verify the block output function before connecting it to any other module
secure belts, rails, gears, and limit switches before powered motion tests
verify switch/button continuity and debounce behavior before using it as an input panel
mount the new build so wires have strain relief and cannot short
record a first-power test, thermal check, and final output function
mechanical_steps: keep strain relief on harvested wires and connectors
mount moving parts so wires cannot touch blades, gears, belts, rails, or hot surfaces
label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification
mark original gear, belt, and limit-switch positions before removing the mechanism
do_not_connect_until: rail voltage is measured under current limit
battery or mains hazards are cleared
